meta.title: Jaguar V12 HE Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
meta.description: Glorious V12 with cooling and ignition fragility — rated from 40+ real UK enquiries. Compare prices from 15+ vetted specialists today.

@graph.name: Jaguar models fitted with the V12 HE

mainEntity.name: How much does a Jaguar V12 HE engine replacement cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned V12 HE typically costs £7,000–£10,000 supply only [JAG-QUOTE], reflecting its rarity, 12-cylinder complexity and specialist-only rebuild requirements. Fitting adds £1,170–£1,560.

mainEntity.name: Is the Jaguar V12 HE a reliable engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: We rate the V12 HE at 2.5★ out of 5 with 75% confidence, based on 40 UK enquiries and specialist reports. Cooling system integrity and ignition reliability are the primary concerns on this ageing V12.

mainEntity.name: What is the most common Jaguar V12 HE failure?

acceptedAnswer.text: Cooling system failures — especially water pump leaks, radiator blockages and thermostat housing cracks — are the most frequent issues, typically from 40,000–60,000 miles, often causing overheating and head gasket damage.

mainEntity.name: Which Jaguar models use the V12 HE engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: The V12 HE was fitted to the XJS, XJ12 and late XJ6 from 1981 to 1997, all in 5.3-litre form with approximately 285–310 PS depending on model and year.

mainEntity.name: How long does the Jaguar V12 HE engine last?

acceptedAnswer.text: With meticulous maintenance and cooling system vigilance, the V12 HE can exceed 150,000 miles. The key to longevity is regular coolant changes and keeping the twin ignition systems in perfect order.

hero.h1: Jaguar V12 HE Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The Jaguar V12 HE is a 5.3-litre petrol V12 — smooth and powerful, but cooling system fragility and ignition complexity are its defining weaknesses. Backed by 40+ real UK enquiries in 2025 [JAG-VERIFIED].

rows.metric: Confidence: 75% confidence — based on 40 UK enquiries, limited specialist rebuild reports and enthusiast community consensus.

rows.value: Complex V12 with dual ignition systems and cooling issues; only a handful of UK specialists will touch it

rows.metric: Some parts still available via specialists and club networks; many components are dealer-only or NLA

rows.value: Recon cost ~50–65% of lowest XJS value (£12,000–£15,000); classics may justify spend

One-line verdict: The V12 HE is a glorious, smooth engine with serious cooling and ignition vulnerabilities — ownership demands specialist maintenance — buy only with a recent cooling system overhaul.

rows.metric: Best for: committed enthusiasts with access to a V12 specialist and a warm garage.
rows.value: Avoid if: you need daily-driver reliability or lack a trusted specialist nearby.

rows.variantBadge: Labour estimate: Fitting a V12 HE typically takes 18–24 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [JAG-QUOTE].
rows.years: CTA line: → Compare V12 HE engine prices from UK specialists
